info.title: Veeam Sizer Calculator API
info.description:
Backend API for the Veeam Sizing Calculator. It calculates infrastructure requirements
(storage, compute, bandwidth) for Veeam backup products; the Blazor frontend
(`calculators-frontend-blazor`) and `veeam.com/calculators` consume it.

### Base path
The base path differs by environment. Locally the API is mounted at the root
(`http://localhost:5015`). On demo/staging/prod it is mounted under `/vse/api`
(via `app.UsePathBase("/vse/api")` in `Program.cs` for any non-Development
environment) — see the server list above for the concrete URLs.

### Authentication
There is no authentication. Access is restricted only via a CORS allowlist
configured in `Program.cs`. This protects browser-based integrations from
unlisted origins, but does **not** restrict server-to-server calls — any
backend that can reach the host can call these endpoints directly.

### Response envelope
- Success: HTTP 200 with `{"success": true, "data": {...}}`.
- Application-level error: HTTP 400 with `{"success": false, "messages": [...]}`
  (a `ReturnMessage`), returned when a calculation class throws or otherwise
  rejects the input.
- Model-validation error: HTTP 400 with a standard ASP.NET Core
  `ValidationProblemDetails` body, returned automatically when a request fails
  `[Range]`-based validation on the input DTO *before* the controller action
  runs.

Both 400 shapes are possible for the same endpoint — callers should handle
both `ReturnMessage` and `ValidationProblemDetails`.

### Common request fields
Many request DTOs share two enums:
- `ProductVersion`: `CurrentVersion` (0) targets v13 sizing rules; `PreviousVersion`
  (-1) targets v12 sizing rules.
- `CalculatorMode`: `Simple` (0) enforces minimum requirements (e.g. min cores,
  min RAM); `Advanced` (1) returns raw calculated values without applying minimums.

post.summary:
Sizes a Veeam Backup & Replication (VBR) backup server deployment: backup server compute
(cores/RAM/disk), the VBR configuration database server, the Enterprise Manager server and its
database, VBR indexing storage, and — for v13 — optional App Plugin and Gateway Server overhead.
post.description:
`ProductVersion` selects between v12 and v13 sizing rules (workload ceiling, minimum cores,
            T-shirt sizing tables, and whether App Plugin/Gateway Server features are available). `Mode`
            controls whether results are floored to `CVariables`' documented minimums (Simple) or returned
            as raw calculated values (Advanced). `DeploymentType` only affects v13 sizing, choosing between
            the Appliance (VSA) and Windows Server disk layouts.

post.summary:
Sizes a Veeam ONE monitoring deployment — the configuration database storage requirement
and the Veeam ONE server's own compute (cores, RAM) — from the VMware, vCloud Director,
Hyper-V, VBR, and/or VB365 infrastructure being monitored, per the sizing formulas
published in Veeam KB2246 and KB4465. `Mode` selects which infrastructure category
drives the database-size calculation and at what data-collection detail level — see
`OneDeploymentMode`.

schema.description:
Input for sizing a Veeam ONE monitoring deployment's configuration database and server
compute requirements. `Mode` selects which infrastructure category (VMware, vCloud
Director, Hyper-V, VBR, or VB365) drives the database-size calculation — populate the
matching nested input(s) for the selected mode. `VmWareInputs`, `VbrInputs`, and
`VB365Input` must all be supplied (non-null, though their fields may be zero) on every
request regardless of `Mode`, because the Veeam ONE server's own compute sizing always
factors VM/host and backup/VB365-server counts.

post.summary:
Sizes an Azure, AWS, or Google Cloud backup appliance. Calculates required object/snapshot
storage capacity and monthly cost for database, VM, and file-share workloads, the
recommended appliance instance size for the chosen cloud provider, and the network
throughput needed by backup worker instances.

schema.description:

independent source-data categories — database/PaaS (`Db*`), VM/instance (`Vm*`), and
file shares (`Files*`) — plus the pricing and compression settings applied across them.
Each category has its own source size, daily change rate, and retention/restore-point settings.

post.summary:
Sizes Securiti SE Download Worker (DW) requirements from data volumes, or the reverse:
scannable capacity from a fixed Download Worker count.
            
Supports two modes, selected by `SizingMode`:
- Forward (default): given structured/unstructured data volumes and a scan timeline, returns
  the Download Workers required plus matching cluster/license recommendations.
- Reverse: given a fixed number of installed Download Workers, returns the data capacity they
  can scan within a timeline.
post.description:
`UnstructuredDocumentMixPercent` and `UnstructuredS3BigDataPercent` must sum to 1.0 —
             a request where they don't is rejected with a 400 before any calculation runs, regardless of
             `SizingMode`. For Forward sizing, structured data can be driven by database count or by
             capacity (`StructuredSizingType`); unstructured data is driven by capacity plus the
             document-mix/S3-big-data profile. The remaining "configurable assumption" fields (tables/
             database, columns/table, DW scan throughput, etc.) apply in both modes and default to the
             values from the Securiti SE Sizing Tool reference spreadsheet.

name.description: Cloud-provider-specific instance/machine type name for the recommended appliance (e.g. "D4s" for Azure, "T3.2xlarge" for AWS, "e2-standard-8" for Google Cloud).

applianceCompute.description: Compute, disk, and network requirements (cores, RAM, volumes) for the recommended appliance instance.

ApplianceSize.description: Recommended cloud backup appliance instance size and its compute requirements.

copyToArchive.description: Whether the archive is a separate copy kept alongside the primary (true, "copy mode") or receives data moved there once primary retention expires (false, "move mode").

Archive.description:
Configuration of the archive copy: where it's stored and, depending on `CopyToArchive`,
whether it's a separate copy kept alongside the primary or data moved there once primary
retention expires.

site.description: Site hosting the archive repository, echoed from the request's `ArchiveCopy.Site`.

repo.description: Repository configuration (storage medium, immutability) echoed from the request's `ArchiveCopy.Repo`.

copyToArchive.description: Whether the archive is configured in copy mode (true) or move mode (false), echoed from the request's `ArchiveCopy.CopyToArchive`.

retentionMonths.description: Archive retention period (months), echoed from the request's `ArchiveCopy.RetentionMonths`.

diskCapacityTB.description: Disk storage capacity required (TB), combining copy-mode and move-mode contributions; non-zero only when `Repo.RepoMedia` is Disk.

objectCapacityTB.description: Object storage capacity required (TB), combining copy-mode and move-mode contributions; non-zero only when `Repo.RepoMedia` is Object.

objPuts.description: Object-storage PUT API call volume; non-zero only when `Repo.RepoMedia` is Object and `CopyToArchive` is true.

network.description: Incremental backup bandwidth requirement and estimated initial full backup duration.

ArchiveRepo.description:
Sizing result for the archive repository, covering both copy mode (a separate archive copy
kept alongside the primary) and move mode (data moved to archive once primary retention
expires).

retentionId.description: Identifier of the retention policy applied to this backup job.

repoId.description: Identifier of the repository this backup job targets.

backupWindowId.description: Identifier of the backup window this job runs within.

Backup.description:
VSE-compatible reference linking a workload's primary backup job to a retention policy,
repository, and backup window defined elsewhere in a multi-workload request, by ID.

retentionId.description: Identifier of the retention policy applied to this backup copy job.

repoId.description: Identifier of the repository this backup copy job targets.

backupWindowId.description: Identifier of the backup window this job runs within.

BackupCopy.description:
VSE-compatible reference linking a workload's backup copy job to a retention policy,
repository, and backup window defined elsewhere in a multi-workload request, by ID.

BackupOptions.description:
- 0 = Backup: This workload's job is a standalone backup job.
- 1 = Copy: This workload's data arrives via a backup copy job — a secondary copy of a backup created elsewhere.
x-enum-descriptions: This workload's job is a standalone backup job.
This workload's data arrives via a backup copy job — a secondary copy of a backup created elsewhere.

workloads.description:
Total number of protected workloads (VMs/instances) backed up by this VBR server. After being
projected forward by `ProjectLength`/`GrowthFactor`, this is the primary driver of the
backup server's own compute sizing: it is looked up against the v12/v13 T-shirt tier tables
(e.g. v13: <=100/500/1000/5000/10000/20000 workloads) to select `VbrServer`'s cores, RAM,
and disk volumes. Also feeds the config-database disk-size and log-storage heuristics directly.

vmsPerJob.description:
Number of workloads packed into each backup job. Only used when `ConcurrentJobs` is 0, to
auto-derive the job count as `workloadsWithGrowth / VmsPerJob`. That job count in turn sizes
the VBR configuration database server (`VBEMDbServer`'s cores/RAM) — it does not affect the
backup server's own compute, which is sized from `Workloads` alone via the T-shirt tables.
Since `ConcurrentJobs` defaults to 10 (non-zero), this field is inert unless the caller
explicitly sets `ConcurrentJobs` to 0.

concurrentJobs.description:
Number of backup jobs running concurrently. When non-zero (the default, 10), this value is used
directly as the job count instead of deriving one from `Workloads`/`VmsPerJob`; set to 0
to auto-derive it instead. Combined with `SingleInstanceJobs`, the resulting job count sizes
the VBR configuration database server (`VBEMDbServer`'s cores/RAM) — not the main backup
server compute, which is sized from `Workloads` alone.

singleInstanceJobs.description:
Additional single-instance (e.g. physical/agent) backup jobs, added on top of `ConcurrentJobs`
(or the job count derived from `Workloads`/`VmsPerJob`) before sizing the VBR
configuration database server (`VBEMDbServer`'s cores/RAM).

pluginCount.description:
V13 App Plugin Backup: number of software appliance app plugins connected to this VBR server.
0 = no plugins; max 200 per V13 spec.

isManagedPlugins.description:
Distinguishes managed (true, max 1,000 DBs) from unmanaged (false, max 10,000 DBs) plugins.
Only evaluated when PluginCount > 0.

databaseCount.description:
Number of databases managed or backed up via the plugins. Used for DB-ceiling validation.
Range enforced against unmanaged upper-bound; managed ceiling checked in controller logic.

gatewayThroughputMBps.description:
Required throughput for the optional Gateway Server that offloads object storage traffic.
0 (default) = no Gateway Server returned in the response.

BackupServerJobConfigs.description:
Job sizing inputs for a VBR backup server: total protected workload count, per-job VM packing,
job/plugin counts, and Gateway Server throughput — drives both the backup server's T-shirt-tier
lookup and the configuration database's compute sizing.

productVersion.description: Which VBR product version to size for — see the enum values for what each version changes.

projectLength.description:
Forecast horizon in years. Combined with `GrowthFactor` to project `JobConfig.Workloads`
forward before it is used for sizing: `workloadsWithGrowth = Workloads * (1 + growthRate * ProjectLength)`.

growthFactor.description:
Annual workload growth rate applied over `ProjectLength` years before sizing. Values >= 1 are
treated as a whole-number percentage (e.g. 10 = 10%/year); values between 0 and 1 are treated as an
already-decimal fraction (e.g. 0.1 = 10%/year). 0 = no growth.

indexingInputs.description:
VBR guest file-system indexing sizing inputs. Shared across product modules — referenced by
`CBackupServerInputObject.IndexingInputs` in the BackupServer (VBR) module and by this
class's own `IndexOptions` property in the VM Agent module.

jobConfig.description: Job sizing inputs for the backup server — see `BackupServerJobConfigs`.

mode.description:
Whether calculated sizing is floored to `CVariables`' documented minimums (Simple) or
returned as raw, unfloored values (Advanced) — see the enum values for details.

deploymentType.description:
VBR backup server OS/disk layout to size. Only affects sizing on v13 — see the enum values for
what each option changes.

CBackupServerInputObject.description:
Request body for `/BackupServer` — sizes a VBR backup server deployment: the backup
server itself, the Enterprise Manager server and its database, the VBR configuration database,
guest file-system indexing, and (v13) the optional Gateway Server.

vbrServer.description:
Compute (cores/RAM/disk volumes) for the VBR backup server itself, sized from
`JobConfig.Workloads` via the v12/v13 T-shirt tier tables, plus v13 App Plugin overhead
when `JobConfig.PluginCount > 0`.

dbServer.description:
Reserved for a standalone VBR database server. Always null in the current implementation — the
VBR configuration database's sizing is reported via `VBEMDbServer` instead.

enterpriseManagerServer.description:
Compute for the Enterprise Manager server and its database, sized from workload count, plus an
index-storage volume entry whose size is driven by `IndexingInputs.GuestSourceFilesMil`/
`EmIndexRetentionMonths` when `IndexingInputs.EmInstalledOnBackupServer` is false (0 GB
when indexing is disabled). Returned as an entirely blank/zeroed object (0 cores, 0 RAM, no
volumes at all) when `EmInstalledOnBackupServer` is true — the corresponding figures are
calculated internally but discarded rather than added to `VbrServer` in the current
implementation.

vbemDbServer.description:
Compute for the VBR configuration database server. Cores/RAM are sized from the total concurrent
job count (`JobConfig.ConcurrentJobs`/`VmsPerJob`/`SingleInstanceJobs`); its disk
volume holds the configuration database.

gatewayServer.description:
V13 Gateway Server for object storage offload.
Null when GatewayThroughputMBps == 0 or ProductVersion != CurrentVersion.

CBackupServerReturnObject.description:
Sizing result for a VBR (Veeam Backup & Replication) backup server deployment: the backup server
itself, the Enterprise Manager server and its database, the VBR configuration database, and — for
v13 with a non-zero `GatewayThroughputMBps` — the optional Gateway Server.

success.description:
Whether the calculation succeeded. Always `true` for a 200 response — failures are
returned as HTTP 400 with a `ReturnMessage` body instead.

data.description: The calculated result payload for this endpoint.

CBackupServerReturnObjectApiResponse.description:
Documentation-only wrapper describing the shape every controller in this API returns on success:
`Ok(new { success = true, data = result })`. It exists purely so Swashbuckle has a concrete
type to generate a response schema from — anonymous C# types cannot be represented in an OpenAPI
schema, so without this wrapper every 200 response documents as bare "OK" with no body.
            
IMPORTANT: this type is NOT referenced by any controller's actual return statement. Controllers
keep constructing the anonymous `{ success, data }` object at runtime; this class is only
ever used inside `[ProducesResponseType(typeof(ApiResponse<T>), 200)]` attributes to
tell Swagger what that anonymous object looks like. Because the two are not linked by the
compiler, they can silently drift apart: if a controller's anonymous success object ever gains,
loses, or renames a property, this type must be updated by hand to match, or the generated
documentation will describe a response shape the API no longer actually returns.
            
Property names are PascalCase here (standard C# convention) but serialize as camelCase
(`success` / `data`) under this project's default System.Text.Json configuration
(`AddControllers()` with no custom `PropertyNamingPolicy`) — the same casing already
produced by the anonymous object in every controller today, so no `[JsonPropertyName]`
attributes are needed for the documented schema to match the real wire format.

mode.description:
Whether calculated sizing is floored to documented minimums (Simple) or returned as raw,
unfloored values (Advanced).

siteId.description:
Caller-supplied identifier for the site the CDP workload belongs to. Not validated or
echoed back in the response.

throughputMBps.description:
Average sustained write (change) rate of the protected workload, in MB/s, before
compression. Drives proxy core count, proxy RAM, and (together with the retention
settings) target storage sizing. Max 9999.

rpoSeconds.description:
Recovery Point Objective, in seconds — the maximum acceptable data loss window. Higher
values increase proxy RAM sizing (more in-flight data must be buffered). Max 9999.

issueTimeSeconds.description:
Extra buffer time, in seconds, to cover a temporary link outage without losing the RPO —
added on top of `RpoSeconds` when sizing the proxy's short-term data cache.
Higher values increase the cache size. Max 9999.

encryptTraffic.description:

a lower per-core rate, so enabling this increases the calculated proxy core count.

sourceTB.description: Total disk size of the protected VM in TB.

shortTermRetentionHours.description: Short term retention window in hours.

issueTimeCacheGB.description:

totalStorageTB.description:

targetProxyCompute.description:

structuredTimelineDays.description: Timeline in days for structured data scanning

unstructuredTimelineDays.description: Timeline in days for unstructured data scanning

throughput given by `DocumentMixGBPerDay`, which is typically higher than the S3/big-data
throughput. Must sum with `UnstructuredS3BigDataPercent` to 1.0.

dataExcludedPercent.description:

values reduce the effective data volume — and therefore the calculated Download Worker

tablesPerDatabase.description: Average number of tables per database

columnsPerTable.description: Average number of columns per table

avgTBPerDatabase.description:

database count for capacity-driven structured sizing, and to convert Download Worker throughput
into a structured TB/day figure for reverse sizing.

dwColumnsPerDay.description: 16vCPU Download Worker daily performance - columns scanned per day

samplingRows.description: Sampling rows for structured data scanning

documentMixGBPerDay.description: Document mix scan performance in GB/day (per 2 DWs)

s3BigDataGBPerDay.description: S3/Big data scan performance in GB/day (per 2 DWs)

availableDownloadWorkers.description: Number of Download Workers available (for reverse sizing)

reverseSizingTimelineDays.description: Scanning timeline in days (for reverse sizing)

structuredVsUnstructuredSplit.description:
Split between structured and unstructured workloads (0-1, where 0.5 = 50% structured)
Used for reverse sizing

(record) backups, combining a full backup of `UsedDataSpaceGB` with incremental
growth and retained incremental generations.

Data Storage and File Storage volumes (`UsedDataSpaceGB` + `UsedFileSpaceGB`).

File backup storage required, in GB — covers the org's Salesforce File Storage
(attachments, Files, Chatter files) backups, combining a 1:1 full backup of
`UsedFileSpaceGB` with growth-projected daily-change increments retained for
`RetentionDays`.

growthRatePercent.description:
Expected annual growth rate of the org's file-change volume, as a percentage (e.g. 2 =
2%/year). Compounded over `GrowthRateScopeYears` to project `FilesDailyChangePercent`'s
daily increment forward before it's retained for `RetentionDays` in `FilesSizeGB`.
0 = no growth.

growthRateScopeYears.description:
Forecast horizon, in years. Scales `ChangedRecordsPerDay`'s contribution to
`DbSizeGB` (extrapolated linearly across the horizon), and the number of years
`GrowthRatePercent` compounds over for `FilesSizeGB`. 0 = no growth projection.

retentionDays.description:
Number of days of VBSF backups to retain (e.g. 30). Scales the retained-incrementals
component of both `DbSizeGB` and `FilesSizeGB`.

backupFrequencyHours.description:
Hours between successive VBSF backup jobs (e.g. 24 = once daily, 4 = six times a day).
Determines how many incremental generations per day are retained in `DbSizeGB`'s
incremental-retention component. Must be greater than 0.

changedRecordsPerDay.description:
Number of Salesforce Data Storage records (rows across standard and custom objects —
Accounts, Contacts, Opportunities, custom objects, etc.) created or modified per day,
across the whole org. Feeds the long-run growth component of `DbSizeGB`, extrapolated
over `GrowthRateScopeYears`.

changedRecordsPerCycle.description:
Number of Salesforce Data Storage records changed within a single backup cycle (the
interval defined by `BackupFrequencyHours`). Sized into the incremental backup image
and retained for `RetentionDays` worth of cycles — scales the retained-incrementals
component of `DbSizeGB`.

usedFileSpaceGB.description:
Current Salesforce org File Storage usage, in GB — the storage category covering
attachments, Files, and Chatter files (Setup > Storage Usage in Salesforce). Sized 1:1
into the full-backup component of `FilesSizeGB`, and contributes (together with
`UsedDataSpaceGB`) to `LogSizeGB`.

usedDataSpaceGB.description:
Current Salesforce org Data Storage usage, in GB — the record-based storage category
covering standard and custom object rows (Accounts, Contacts, Opportunities, etc.; Setup
> Storage Usage in Salesforce). Sized into the full-backup component of `DbSizeGB`,
and contributes (together with `UsedFileSpaceGB`) to `LogSizeGB`.

filesDailyChangePercent.description:
Percentage of the org's File Storage that changes per day (e.g. 2 = 2%/day; typical
default is 2%). Scales the daily incremental-growth component of `FilesSizeGB` before
it's compounded by `GrowthRatePercent` and retained for `RetentionDays`.

proxyCompute.description: Compute (cores/RAM) and network throughput required for the backup proxy that reads data from the source and writes it to the repository.

repoCompute.description: Compute (cores/RAM), network throughput, and disk volumes required for the backup repository — includes the performance, capacity, and archive tier disk volumes.

indexResults.description: Guest file-system indexing compute and storage requirements, when `IndexOptions.enabled` is true; zeroed out otherwise.

transactions.description: Object storage API transaction (PUT call) volume for the performance, capacity, and archive tiers, when the corresponding tier is object storage.

workspaceGB.description: Reserved workspace/merge-area capacity, in GB, for backup chain operations on the performance tier repository. Always 0 when the performance tier is object storage.

performanceTierImmutabilityTaxGB.description: Additional storage, in GB, reserved on the performance tier to satisfy immutability retention when `immutablePerf` is enabled. 0 otherwise.

capacityTierImmutabilityTaxGB.description: Additional storage, in GB, reserved on the capacity tier to satisfy immutability retention when `immutableCap` is enabled. 0 otherwise.

restorePoints.description: Per-restore-point breakdown across the performance, capacity, and archive tiers — one entry per simulated restore point, with its size and GFS/immutability flags.

sourceTB.description: Source data volume, in TB, to be replicated. The primary driver of target datastore and proxy sizing.

changeRate.description: Daily change rate, as a percentage of source data, used to size the incremental data replicated at each restore point.

sizing the network bandwidth needed to replicate incremental changes. Does not reduce
`TargetDataStoreSpaceRequiredTB`, which is sized from uncompressed source data.

CompressLevel.description:
Assumed data-compression ratio applied to a tape drive's native capacity when estimating how
much backup data it can hold (used by both Tape and FileToTape sizing). Ignored whenever the
input's `CustomCompression` value is set above 1 — that value overrides this enum entirely.

- 0 = None: No compression assumed — effective tape capacity equals the drive's native (uncompressed) capacity.
- 1 = Medium: Moderate compression assumed — multiplies native tape capacity by 1.5x (LTO4/LTO5) or 1.875x (LTO6 and newer, TS1140/TS1150/TS1155).
- 2 = High: Maximum compression assumed — multiplies native tape capacity by 2x (LTO4/LTO5) or 2.5x (LTO6 and newer, TS1140/TS1150/TS1155).

incremental backup sizes forward before they're sized. 0 = no growth.

sizeOfFullBackupTB.description: Size of one full backup, in TB (pre-compression).

incSizeGB.description: Size of one incremental backup, in GB (pre-compression).

logGenerationRate.description: Rate at which transaction logs are generated on the source database.

logRetentionPoints.description: Number of transaction-log restore points to retain.

logSizeGB.description:
Average daily transaction log volume, in GB. Internally converted to a weekly figure
(multiplied by 7) and combined with the full/incremental weekly footprint before
`WeeksRetention` is applied.

compressionPercent.description:
Percentage the backup data is reduced by compression (compress BY, not compress TO — e.g. 50
means the stored size is half the source size). Applied to full, incremental, and log volumes,
and to the outbound bandwidth estimate.

weeksRetention.description:
Number of weeks of full + incremental + log backups to retain. Multiplies the combined weekly
backup footprint to produce the base retained capacity, before any GFS/month-end long-term
points are added.

changeRatePercent.description: Daily data change rate, as a percentage.

monthEndRetention.description:
Number of extra long-term full-backup copies retained per month, on top of
`WeeksRetention`. When set (non-null, non-zero), this takes the place of
`Retention.Gfs` entirely — the two are mutually exclusive; whichever is set is used, and
`Retention.Gfs` is ignored if this is set. Not applied when `DbType` is `Sap`.

retention.description:
Grandfather-Father-Son long-term retention points (`Gfs.Weeks`/`Months`/`Years`)
added on top of `WeeksRetention`'s weekly total, each worth one extra full-backup copy.
Only used when `MonthEndRetention` is null or zero, and not applied when `DbType` is
`Sap`.

backupWindow.description:
Hours available to complete a backup. Used to derive the required network throughput
(outbound Mbps) sized into the plugin host's and repository's compute requirements.

numberOfBackupChannels.description:
Number of parallel backup channels run per instance. Combined with `DbInstanceCount` to
size the plugin host's total cores (RAM follows at roughly 1 GB per 5 channels).

immutability.description: Whether backups are stored immutably (protected against modification/deletion for `ImmutablePeriodDays`).

immutablePeriodDays.description: Immutability retention-lock period, in days. Only meaningful when `Immutability` is enabled.

DeploymentType.description:

enabled.description: Whether guest file-system indexing sizing is turned on. When false, the underlying calculation produces an entirely zeroed result (0 cores, 0 RAM, no volumes, 0 GB on both space figures). When true, `GuestSourceFilesMil`/`EmIndexRetentionMonths` drive a non-zero result instead. In the BackupServer module, only the resulting EM space figure ever reaches the response — as the Enterprise Manager server's own index-storage volume, and only when `EmInstalledOnBackupServer` is false; this setting never affects the VBR backup server's own compute or storage. In the VmAgent module (which reuses this same input type via its own `IndexOptions` property), the full computed result — compute (cores/RAM), volumes, and both space figures — is returned verbatim as `data.indexResults`, regardless of `EmInstalledOnBackupServer`; there is no discarding step in that code path.

guestSourceFilesMil.description: Millions of guest files indexed per restore point. Scales the per-restore-point index size (50 MB uncompressed / 12.5 MB compressed per million files), which is the basis for both of the underlying calculation's space figures (see `EmInstalledOnBackupServer`). In the BackupServer module, only the EM-server space figure ever surfaces in the response — as the Enterprise Manager server's own index-storage volume, and only when `EmInstalledOnBackupServer` is false; the other figure is computed but discarded. In the VmAgent module, both figures surface directly in `data.indexResults` (as `vbrServerSpaceGB`/`emServerSpaceGB` and as matching volume entries).

emIndexRetentionMonths.description: Months of index data assumed retained on the Enterprise Manager server. Multiplied by the compressed per-restore-point index size (times 30 days/month), plus a two-day allowance for uncompressed index data, to compute the figure that lands in whichever of the underlying calculation's two space outputs `EmInstalledOnBackupServer` selects for the EM server. In the BackupServer module this only surfaces in the response via the Enterprise Manager server's own index-storage volume, and only when `EmInstalledOnBackupServer` is false. In the VmAgent module the resulting figure surfaces directly in `data.indexResults` regardless of `EmInstalledOnBackupServer` (see that property for which field it lands in).

emInstalledOnBackupServer.description: Whether the Enterprise Manager role is (nominally) co-installed on the backup server. Flips which of the underlying calculation's two space figures receives the EM-index size driven by `GuestSourceFilesMil`/`EmIndexRetentionMonths`: when true, that figure lands in the "VBR server" slot (and the "EM server" slot is 0); when false, it lands in the "EM server" slot instead (and the "VBR server" slot instead reflects `RetentionPointsSumOfRegularAndGfs`). In the BackupServer module, when true, the response's `EnterpriseManagerServer` is returned as an entirely blank/zeroed object — 0 cores, 0 RAM, no volume entries at all (not even a 0 GB placeholder). The corresponding figures are calculated internally but are not added to `VbrServer`; they are simply discarded. When false, `EnterpriseManagerServer` reports its own baseline compute (from workload count) plus an install-space volume and an index-storage volume sized from `GuestSourceFilesMil`/`EmIndexRetentionMonths` (0 GB when indexing is disabled). In the VmAgent module there is no equivalent zeroing step: `data.indexResults` always reports the full computed compute/volumes/space figures regardless of this setting — only which of `vbrServerSpaceGB`/`emServerSpaceGB` ends up non-zero changes.

retentionPointsSumOfRegularAndGfs.description: Total restore points to index — nominally the sum of regular (performance-tier) and GFS restore points. In the BackupServer module, this value feeds the "VBR server" space figure in the underlying calculation (used only when `EmInstalledOnBackupServer` is false) — but that figure is computed and then discarded before reaching the response, so this input has no observable effect there. In the VmAgent module, this property is not read at all — that module derives its own retention count from its own daily/weekly/monthly/yearly retention inputs and uses that instead. This input is inert in both modules, for different reasons.

siteName.description:
Caller-supplied display name for the site or location this input represents. For the
caller's own reference; not used in the sizing calculation.

id.description:
Caller-supplied identifier for this specific input. For the caller's own reference; not
used in the sizing calculation.

name.description:
Caller-supplied display name for this input. For the caller's own reference; not used in
the sizing calculation.

growthRatePercent.description:
Expected annual data growth rate, as a percentage (e.g. 10 = 10%/year). Compounded over
`GrowthRateScopeYears` to project each object group's source capacity forward before
sizing — higher values increase the resulting storage requirement.

growthRateScopeYears.description:
Number of years `GrowthRatePercent` is compounded over when projecting future storage
needs. 0 disables growth projection, so each object group's source capacity is used as-is.

changeUnit.description: Time unit associated with the aggregate data-change figures for this deployment.

objectStorage.description:
Whether the backup repository is object storage (e.g. S3, Azure Blob) rather than a
disk-based repository. When true, the returned `TotalCapacity` reflects object-storage
sizing and `TotalCacheTB` is populated. When false, `TotalCapacity` instead reflects
disk-based sizing — which includes additional JET database working-space overhead — and
`TotalCacheTB` is 0.

objects.description:
The Microsoft 365 objects being protected, grouped by workload type (Mailbox/Archive/Team/Site).
At least one entry is required. Drives all proxy, controller, repository storage, and API
transaction sizing in the response.

newMath.description:
Opts into an alternate, still-evolving sizing model (VB365 v8+ "T-shirt" sizing) that derives only
proxy/controller requirements from total object count, in place of the default per-object capacity
and API-transaction calculations described elsewhere on this type. Currently well-defined only for
smaller deployments; leave false to use the standard calculation.

M365InputObject.description:
Sizing input for a Veeam Backup for Microsoft 365 deployment: growth/change assumptions plus
one or more groups of protected Exchange, SharePoint, and Teams objects.

siteId.description: Identifier for the site/request this result corresponds to.

siteName.description: Display name for the site/request this result corresponds to.

id.description: Identifier for this result.

name.description: Display name for this result.

totalCapacity.description:
Total backup repository storage required, in TB. Reflects object-storage sizing when the
request's `ObjectStorage` is true, or disk-based sizing (which includes JET database
working-space overhead) when it is false.

totalCacheTB.description:
Additional object-storage repository cache space required, in TB. Only populated when the
request's `ObjectStorage` is true; 0 for disk-based repository sizing.

proxyConfig.description: Compute (cores/RAM) required for the VB365 backup proxy role(s).

proxiesRequired.description: Number of backup proxy servers required.

controllerCompute.description: Compute (cores/RAM) required for the VB365 controller/backup server role.

databaseCompute.description: Compute and storage required for the VB365 configuration/restore-point database.

natsCompute.description: Compute required for the VB365 NATS messaging service.

repository.description: Storage volume(s) required for the backup repository, in the underlying `Volumes` disk entries.

firstMonthTransactions.description:
Estimated Microsoft Graph API transaction volume (in thousands) for the first month of backups,
when the full data set plus one month of change is being transferred.

lastMonthTransactions.description:
Estimated steady-state Microsoft Graph API transaction volume (in thousands) for a typical
month once only incremental changes are being transferred.

M365ReturnObject.description:
Sizing result for a Veeam Backup for Microsoft 365 deployment: repository storage, proxy and
controller compute, database/NATS service compute, and estimated Microsoft Graph API transaction
volume for the objects supplied in the request.

success.description:
Whether the calculation succeeded. Always `true` for a 200 response — failures are
returned as HTTP 400 with a `ReturnMessage` body instead.

data.description: The calculated result payload for this endpoint.

M365ReturnObjectApiResponse.description:
Documentation-only wrapper describing the shape every controller in this API returns on success:
`Ok(new { success = true, data = result })`. It exists purely so Swashbuckle has a concrete
type to generate a response schema from — anonymous C# types cannot be represented in an OpenAPI
schema, so without this wrapper every 200 response documents as bare "OK" with no body.
            
IMPORTANT: this type is NOT referenced by any controller's actual return statement. Controllers
keep constructing the anonymous `{ success, data }` object at runtime; this class is only
ever used inside `[ProducesResponseType(typeof(ApiResponse<T>), 200)]` attributes to
tell Swagger what that anonymous object looks like. Because the two are not linked by the
compiler, they can silently drift apart: if a controller's anonymous success object ever gains,
loses, or renames a property, this type must be updated by hand to match, or the generated
documentation will describe a response shape the API no longer actually returns.
            
Property names are PascalCase here (standard C# convention) but serialize as camelCase
(`success` / `data`) under this project's default System.Text.Json configuration
(`AddControllers()` with no custom `PropertyNamingPolicy`) — the same casing already
produced by the anonymous object in every controller today, so no `[JsonPropertyName]`
attributes are needed for the documented schema to match the real wire format.

incMBperS.description: Incremental backup throughput required (MB/s) to complete a daily incremental within the backup window.

fullMinutes.description: Estimated time (minutes) to complete the initial full backup at the incremental throughput rate above.

NetworkReq.description: Network bandwidth needed to complete backups within the configured backup window.

objPutsMonth1.description: PUT calls (thousands) in the first month, covering the initial full backup plus that month's incrementals.

objPutsFinal.description: Steady-state PUT calls (thousands) per month once retention is fully populated.

ObjectAPIcalls.description: Object-storage PUT API call volume (in thousands) for a repository, split by billing period.

objectCount.description:
Number of objects of this type being protected (e.g. number of mailboxes, sites, or teams).
Drives proxy/controller sizing — where each Team counts as two, since a Team combines an
Exchange mailbox and a SharePoint site — and the restore-point database size estimate.

capacityTb.description:
Current source data size for this group of objects, in TB. The base capacity that growth
and change-rate projections are applied to.

changePercent.description:
Daily rate of data change (churn) for this group, as a percentage of `CapacityTb`.
Higher values increase the projected incremental backup size used when estimating storage.

retentionPeriod.description:
Number of days of backups to retain for this group of objects. Longer retention increases
the projected storage requirement.

compressByPercent.description:
Percentage by which this group's backup data is reduced through compression — "compress BY",
not "compress TO" (e.g. 50 means the data ends up at roughly half its original size).

itemCount.description: Number of individual items (e.g. messages or files) contained within these objects.

ObjectAttributes.description: Sizing attributes for one group of same-type Microsoft 365 objects (see `Vb365_Object.Type`).

objCapacityMonth1TB.description: Object storage required (TB) at the end of the first month.

objCapacityFinalTB.description: Steady-state object storage required (TB) once the full retention window is populated.

ObjectCapacity.description: Object storage repository capacity, tracked at two points in time because capacity grows as retention fills up.

firstMonthTransactions.description: API PUT calls, in thousands, during the first month of the retention window.

secondMonthTransactions.description: API PUT calls, in thousands, during the second month of the retention window.

finalMonthTransactions.description: API PUT calls, in thousands, during the final (steady-state) month of the retention window.

ObjectTransactions.description: API transaction (PUT call) counts, in thousands, for the first, second, and final month of the retention window.

OneDeploymentMode.description:
Which infrastructure category Veeam ONE monitors and how detailed its performance-data
collection is — determines which nested input group(s) on `VeeamOneInput` drive the
configuration database-size calculation. See the enum values for what each mode changes.

- 0 = Typical: Full virtual and backup infrastructure monitoring — sizes the database from VmWareInputs, VcdInputs, HvInputs, and VbrInputs together, at Veeam ONE's standard (higher-frequency) performance-data collection cadence. The default, most common deployment profile.
- 1 = Advanced: Same infrastructure scope as Typical, but assumes a lower-frequency performance-data collection cadence, yielding a smaller estimated database size than Typical for identical counts.
- 2 = BackupOnly: Monitors only the VBR backup infrastructure (Enterprise Manager, backup servers, repositories, proxies, tape and copy jobs), plus coarse VMware/Hyper-V VM and host counts, rather than full virtual-infrastructure inventory detail.
- 3 = Vb365Typical: Monitors a Veeam Backup for Microsoft 365 deployment instead of virtual/backup infrastructure, sizing the database from VB365Input at Veeam ONE's standard (higher-estimate) cadence.
- 4 = Vb365Advanced: Same scope as Vb365Typical, but yields a smaller estimated database size than Vb365Typical for identical counts.
x-enum-descriptions: Full virtual and backup infrastructure monitoring — sizes the database from VmWareInputs, VcdInputs, HvInputs, and VbrInputs together, at Veeam ONE's standard (higher-frequency) performance-data collection cadence. The default, most common deployment profile.
Same infrastructure scope as Typical, but assumes a lower-frequency performance-data collection cadence, yielding a smaller estimated database size than Typical for identical counts.

monthlyCapacityTbInitial.description: Year-1 baseline VM snapshot-storage capacity (TB), before `GrowthRatePercent` is applied.

snapResults.description: VM backup sizing for the snapshot-storage tier (AWS/Google Cloud's primary tier; also computed for Azure).

repo.description: This copy's repository configuration (storage medium, immutability).

retentionDays.description: Number of daily restore points to retain on this copy's repository. Minimum 1.

incBackupWindowHours.description: Length (hours) of the daily backup window for this copy, used to size incremental backup throughput and (for the primary copy) proxy compute. Must be greater than 0.

Primary.description:

window settings.

site.description: Site hosting this repository — echoed from the corresponding copy configuration in the request (`PrimaryCopy` for the primary repository; the matching entry in `SecondaryCopies` for a secondary).

repo.description: Repository configuration (storage medium, immutability), echoed from the corresponding copy's `Repo` setting in the request.

retentionDays.description: Number of daily restore points retained, echoed from the corresponding copy's `RetentionDays` setting in the request.

incBackupWindowHours.description: Backup window length (hours) used to size incremental throughput, echoed from the corresponding copy's `IncBackupWindowHours` setting in the request.

diskCapacityTB.description: Disk storage capacity required (TB); non-zero only when `Repo.RepoMedia` is Disk.

objectCapacityTB.description: Object storage capacity required (TB); non-zero only when `Repo.RepoMedia` is Object.

objPuts.description: Object-storage PUT API call volume; non-zero only when `Repo.RepoMedia` is Object.

network.description: Incremental backup bandwidth requirement and estimated initial full backup duration.

PrimaryRepo.description:
Sizing result for the primary backup repository: site, repository settings, retention,
capacity, API call volume, and network requirements.

ProductVersion.description:
Specifies the available product versions for the VM Agent. Also used by the BackupServer
(VBR) input DTO, where it selects between v12 and v13 sizing rules.

- 0 = CurrentVersion: v13 sizing rules: 20k workload limit, 8-core minimum, higher specs; on BackupServer this also honors DeploymentType (Appliance vs WindowsServer T-shirt sizing).
- -1 = PreviousVersion: v12 sizing rules: 10k workload limit, 4-core minimum, ignores DeploymentType (BackupServer always uses the v12 T-shirt layout).

v12 sizing rules: 10k workload limit, 4-core minimum, ignores DeploymentType (BackupServer always uses the v12 T-shirt layout).

compute.description: Cores, RAM, network throughput, and (for repository roles) disk volumes required.

siteId.description: Site or role identifier this compute requirement is associated with. Left empty for VM Agent; VM Replication sets it to a fixed role label ("source" or "Target") rather than a real site identifier.

ProxyResult.description: Compute (cores/RAM/network throughput) and, where applicable, disk volumes required for a proxy or repository role.

siteId.description:
Caller-supplied identifier for this site/workload entry. Not validated or echoed back in
the response.

siteName.description:
Caller-supplied display name for this site/workload entry. Not validated or echoed back
in the response.

id.description:
Caller-supplied identifier for this sizing entry. Not validated or echoed back in the
response.

name.description:
Caller-supplied display name for this sizing entry. Not validated or echoed back in the
response.

growthRatePercent.description:
Annual growth rate (%) applied uniformly to the database, VM, and file source sizes when
projecting the Year-N ("projected") capacity and cost figures over
`GrowthRateScopeYears`. 0 = no growth.

backupWindowHours.description:
Length of the backup window, in hours. Used to size the inbound/outbound network
throughput reported in `WorkerCompute` for moving the daily DB+VM incremental data
within this window. Default 8; maximum 336 (2 weeks).

growthRateScopeYears.description:
Forecast horizon, in years, over which `GrowthRatePercent` compounds to produce the
Year-N capacity/cost figures for DB, VM, and file backups.

dbSourceTb.description: Source data size (TB) for the database/PaaS workload being backed up.

dbChangeRatePercent.description: Daily data change rate (%) for the database workload, used to size the incremental portion of DB backup capacity.

dbRestorePoints.description:
Number of daily restore points for DB backups. Only affects the immutability retention
window applied to DB storage when `Immutability` is enabled; DB capacity itself is
sized from `DbRestorePointsObject`/`DbRestorePointsSnap` or, when those are 0,
from `DbObjectRetention`/`DbSnapRetention`.

dbRestorePointsObject.description:
Flat count of daily restore points to keep for the DB object-storage tier. When non-zero,
this (together with `DbRestorePointsSnap`) replaces `DbObjectRetention` for
capacity sizing; when 0, `DbObjectRetention`/`DbSnapRetention` are used instead.

dbRestorePointsSnap.description:
Flat count of daily restore points to keep for the DB snapshot tier. Only used when
`DbRestorePointsObject` is also non-zero (see `DbRestorePointsObject`).

dbObjectRetention.description: Day/GFS retention policy governing the DB object-storage tier, used when `DbRestorePointsObject` is 0.

dbSnapRetention.description: Day/GFS retention policy governing the DB snapshot tier, used when `DbRestorePointsObject` is 0.

vmRegion.description: Cloud region identifier for the VM workload (e.g. "uswest"), used to select region-specific instance pricing/placement.

vmSourceTb.description: Source data size (TB) for the VM/instance workload being backed up.

vmChangeRatePercent.description: Daily data change rate (%) for the VM workload, used to size the incremental portion of VM backup capacity.

vmRestorePointsObject.description:
Flat count of daily restore points to keep for the VM object-storage tier, intended to
override `VmObjectRetention`'s day/GFS counts (parallel to `DbRestorePointsObject`).

vmObjectRetention.description: Day/GFS retention policy governing the VM object-storage tier.

vmSnapRetention.description: Day/GFS retention policy governing the VM snapshot-storage tier.

filesSourceTb.description: Source data size (TB) for the file-share workload being backed up.

filesChangeRatePercent.description: Daily data change rate (%) for the file-share workload, used to size the incremental portion of file backup capacity.

filesRestorePoints.description:
Flat count of daily restore points to keep for file backups. When non-zero, replaces
`FilesRetention`'s day/GFS counts for capacity sizing.

filesRetention.description: Day/GFS retention policy governing file backups, used when `FilesRestorePoints` is 0.

objectPricePerGB.description:
Price per GB per month for object-tier storage (e.g. Azure Blob). Drives Azure DB and VM
object-tier backup cost, and the VM object-tier cost figures on AWS and Google Cloud.

snapshotPricePerGB.description:
Price per GB per month for snapshot-tier storage. Drives AWS/Google Cloud DB backup cost,
VM snapshot-tier cost across all providers, and file backup cost (files are always billed
at the snapshot-storage rate).

expectedInstanceCount.description:
Expected number of VM/instance workloads to be protected by this appliance. Drives the
recommended appliance size tier (`RecommendedApplianceSize`) selected for the chosen
`Type`.

type.description:
Target cloud provider for this appliance sizing request. Determines which
compression/pricing rules and appliance size tiers are applied throughout the calculation
— see the enum values for provider-specific behavior.

compressionPercent.description:
Percentage the source data is compressed by before capacity/cost calculation (compress
BY, not compress TO — 50 means the compressed size is half of the original). Applies to
Azure DB backups and to VM object-tier backups on Azure and AWS; Google Cloud's VM/DB
paths and all snapshot-tier calculations use a fixed reduction and ignore this field.
Default 50.

immutability.description:
Enables immutable (WORM) storage retention. When true, an immutability retention window
is applied to the calculated capacity/cost and transaction figures for the applicable
workloads.

PublicCloudInputs.description:
Sizing request for a single Azure, AWS, or Google Cloud backup appliance. Covers up to three
independent source-data categories — database/PaaS (`Db*`), VM/instance (`Vm*`), and
file shares (`Files*`) — plus the pricing and compression settings applied across them.
Each category has its own source size, daily change rate, and retention/restore-point settings.

siteId.description: Identifier for the site this result belongs to. Not populated from the request — always a freshly generated value.

siteName.description: Display name for the site this result belongs to. Not populated from the request — always empty.

id.description: Identifier for this sizing entry. Not populated from the request — always a freshly generated value.

name.description: Display name for this sizing entry. Not populated from the request — always empty.

dbCapacityTb.description: Total required storage capacity (TB) for the database workload, including compression (where applicable), growth projection, and any immutability overhead.

dbCost.description: Monthly cost of DB backup storage.

dbCostSnap.description: Monthly cost of DB backup storage sized from the DB snapshot-tier retention settings (`DbSnapRetention`/`DbRestorePointsSnap`); billed at Azure's object-storage rate, or the snapshot-storage rate for AWS and Google Cloud.

dbCostObject.description: Monthly cost of DB backup storage sized from the DB object-tier retention settings (`DbObjectRetention`/`DbRestorePointsObject`); billed at Azure's object-storage rate, or the snapshot-storage rate for AWS and Google Cloud.

dbMonthlyCapacityTbInitialSnap.description: Year-1 baseline storage capacity (TB) for the DB snapshot tier, before `GrowthRatePercent` is applied.

dbMonthlyCapacityTbProjectedSnap.description: Projected storage capacity (TB) for the DB snapshot tier after applying `GrowthRatePercent` over `GrowthRateScopeYears`.

dbMonthlyCostInitialSnap.description: Year-1 baseline monthly cost for the DB snapshot tier, before growth is applied.

dbMonthlyCostProjectedSnap.description: Projected monthly cost for the DB snapshot tier after applying the configured growth rate.

dbMonthlyCapacityTbInitialObject.description: Year-1 baseline storage capacity (TB) for the DB object tier, before `GrowthRatePercent` is applied.

dbMonthlyCapacityTbProjectedObject.description: Projected storage capacity (TB) for the DB object tier after applying `GrowthRatePercent` over `GrowthRateScopeYears`.

dbMonthlyCostInitialObject.description: Year-1 baseline monthly cost for the DB object tier, before growth is applied.

dbMonthlyCostProjectedObject.description: Projected monthly cost for the DB object tier after applying the configured growth rate.

dbApiCalls.description: Estimated number of object-storage API transactions for the first DB backup cycle (uses the immutability-adjusted figure from `DbTransactions` when `Immutability` is enabled).

dbTransactions.description: Per-cycle breakdown of estimated DB backup API transaction counts (first/second/steady-state, with immutability-enabled variants).

vmApiCalls.description: Estimated number of object-storage API transactions for the first VM backup cycle (uses the immutability-adjusted figure from `VmTransactions` when `Immutability` is enabled).

vmTransactions.description: Per-cycle breakdown of estimated VM backup API transaction counts (first/second/steady-state, with immutability-enabled variants).

fileCapacityTb.description: Total required storage capacity (TB) for the file-share workload, including growth projection.

fileCost.description: Monthly cost of file backup storage, billed at the snapshot-storage rate.

fileMonthlyCapacityTbInitial.description: Year-1 baseline storage capacity (TB) for file backups, before `GrowthRatePercent` is applied.

fileMonthlyCapacityTbProjected.description: Projected storage capacity (TB) for file backups after applying `GrowthRatePercent` over `GrowthRateScopeYears`.

fileMonthlyCostInitial.description: Year-1 baseline monthly cost for file backups, before growth is applied.

fileMonthlyCostProjected.description: Projected monthly cost for file backups after applying the configured growth rate.

fileApiCalls.description: Estimated number of object-storage API transactions for the first file backup cycle (uses the immutability-adjusted figure from `FileTransactions` when `Immutability` is enabled).

fileTransactions.description: Per-cycle breakdown of estimated file backup API transaction counts (first/second/steady-state, with immutability-enabled variants).

recommendedApplianceSize.description: Recommended backup appliance instance for the target cloud provider (`Type`), selected from a fixed per-provider tier table keyed on `ExpectedInstanceCount`.

vmResults.description: VM backup sizing results broken out by storage tier (object vs. snapshot).

workerCompute.description: Network throughput (inbound/outbound Mbps) required by the backup worker instances that move the daily DB+VM incremental data within `BackupWindowHours`.

PublicCloudReturnObject.description:
Sizing result for a single Azure, AWS, or Google Cloud backup appliance request: required
storage capacity and monthly cost for each source-data category (database, VM, files), the
recommended appliance instance size, and the compute/network requirements for backup workers.

PublicCloudType.description:
Target public cloud provider for a `PublicCloudInputs` sizing request. Selects which
pricing tier, compression behavior, and appliance instance-size table are used.

- 0 = Azure: Microsoft Azure. Backups use object-storage pricing/tiers, with compression applied per CompressionPercent; the recommended appliance is an Azure VM size (e.g. B2s, D4s, D8s_v3).
- 1 = Amazon: Amazon Web Services (AWS). Backups use snapshot-storage pricing/tiers with no compression applied; the recommended appliance is an EC2 instance type (e.g. T3.medium, T3.2xlarge, C4.9xlarge).
- 2 = Google: Google Cloud Platform. Backups are costed using GCP-specific fixed compression and disk-occupancy assumptions rather than CompressionPercent; the recommended appliance is a Compute Engine machine type (e.g. e2-highmem-2, e2-standard-4/8/16).
x-enum-descriptions: Microsoft Azure. Backups use object-storage pricing/tiers, with compression applied per CompressionPercent; the recommended appliance is an Azure VM size (e.g. B2s, D4s, D8s_v3).
Amazon Web Services (AWS). Backups use snapshot-storage pricing/tiers with no compression applied; the recommended appliance is an EC2 instance type (e.g. T3.medium, T3.2xlarge, C4.9xlarge).

Google Cloud Platform. Backups are costed using GCP-specific fixed compression and disk-occupancy assumptions rather than CompressionPercent; the recommended appliance is a Compute Engine machine type (e.g. e2-highmem-2, e2-standard-4/8/16).

RepoMedia.description:
Storage medium backing a repository (primary, secondary, or archive).

- 0 = Disk: Local or attached disk storage (e.g. a Linux/Windows repository server's local or attached volumes).
- 1 = Object: S3-compatible object storage, on-premises or in the cloud.
x-enum-descriptions: Local or attached disk storage (e.g. a Linux/Windows repository server's local or attached volumes).
S3-compatible object storage, on-premises or in the cloud.

repoMedia.description: Storage medium for this repository — see the enum values for what each means.

immutability.description: Whether backups on this repository are made immutable for `ImmutabilityDays`. Extends the effective retention window used for capacity sizing.

immutabilityDays.description: Number of days backups stay immutable, added to retention when computing repository capacity. Only applied when `Immutability` is true. Typical range 0–90; longer periods are accepted but flagged as costly.

Repository.description:
Configuration of a single backup repository (primary, secondary, or archive): its identity,
storage medium, and immutability settings.

days.description: Number of daily restore points to retain.

gfs.description: Grandfather-Father-Son (weekly/monthly/yearly) retention on top of the daily points.

Simple + GFS retention policy shared by input DTOs across multiple product modules
(currently the Public Cloud and Database Plugin calculators — see `PublicCloudInputs`
and `DbPluginInputObject`).

success.description:
Always `false` when this type is returned — a successful calculation never uses
`ReturnMessage`, it returns the module-specific result object instead.

messages.description:
One or more human-readable error messages explaining why the request could not be processed
(e.g. validation failures or unhandled exception text).

ReturnMessage.description:
Standard error-response body returned by every module's controller when a request fails, via
`BadRequest(new ReturnMessage { Success = false, Messages = [...] })` (HTTP 400).

retentionDays.description: Number of daily restore points to retain on this copy's repository. Minimum 1.

incBackupWindowHours.description: Length (hours) of the daily backup window for this copy, used to size incremental backup throughput and (for the primary copy) proxy compute. Must be greater than 0.

Secondary.description:
Configuration of one secondary (backup copy) job — same shape as `Primary`. Supply one
entry in `UnstructuredInputObject.SecondaryCopies` per secondary repository; entries with
`RetentionDays` of 0 are skipped when sizing.

site.description: Site hosting this repository — echoed from the corresponding copy configuration in the request (`PrimaryCopy` for the primary repository; the matching entry in `SecondaryCopies` for a secondary).

repo.description: Repository configuration (storage medium, immutability), echoed from the corresponding copy's `Repo` setting in the request.

retentionDays.description: Number of daily restore points retained, echoed from the corresponding copy's `RetentionDays` setting in the request.

incBackupWindowHours.description: Backup window length (hours) used to size incremental throughput, echoed from the corresponding copy's `IncBackupWindowHours` setting in the request.

diskCapacityTB.description: Disk storage capacity required (TB); non-zero only when `Repo.RepoMedia` is Disk.

objectCapacityTB.description: Object storage capacity required (TB); non-zero only when `Repo.RepoMedia` is Object.

objPuts.description: Object-storage PUT API call volume; non-zero only when `Repo.RepoMedia` is Object.

network.description: Incremental backup bandwidth requirement and estimated initial full backup duration.

SecondaryRepo.description:
Sizing result for one configured secondary (backup copy) repository — same shape as
`PrimaryRepo`; one instance is returned per entry in the request's `SecondaryCopies`.

configurationName.description: Name of the cluster configuration (e.g., "16vCPU Single Node", "Cluster: 2x 16vCPU Nodes")

downloadWorkerCapacity.description: Number of Download Workers this configuration supports

quantityNeeded.description: Number of this cluster type needed to handle the total DW requirement

totalDownloadWorkersProvided.description: Total Download Workers provided by this recommendation

totalVCpu.description: Total vCPU required for this recommendation

SecuritiSizingMode.description:
Sizing direction mode

- 0 = Forward: Given structured/unstructured data volumes and a target scan timeline, calculates the Download Workers required plus matching cluster/license recommendations. Ignores AvailableDownloadWorkers, ReverseSizingTimelineDays, and StructuredVsUnstructuredSplit.
- 1 = Reverse: Given a fixed number of Download Workers, calculates the data capacity (TB) they can scan within a target timeline. Reads AvailableDownloadWorkers, ReverseSizingTimelineDays, and StructuredVsUnstructuredSplit, plus the document-mix/S3-big-data percentages and the configurable throughput assumptions (DWColumnsPerDay, TablesPerDatabase, ColumnsPerTable, AvgTBPerDatabase, DocumentMixGBPerDay, S3BigDataGBPerDay); ignores DatabaseCount, StructuredCapacityTB, StructuredTimelineDays, UnstructuredCapacityTB, UnstructuredTimelineDays, DataExcludedPercent, and FullScanPercent.
x-enum-descriptions: Given structured/unstructured data volumes and a target scan timeline, calculates the Download Workers required plus matching cluster/license recommendations. Ignores AvailableDownloadWorkers, ReverseSizingTimelineDays, and StructuredVsUnstructuredSplit.

Given a fixed number of Download Workers, calculates the data capacity (TB) they can scan within a target timeline. Reads AvailableDownloadWorkers, ReverseSizingTimelineDays, and StructuredVsUnstructuredSplit, plus the document-mix/S3-big-data percentages and the configurable throughput assumptions (DWColumnsPerDay, TablesPerDatabase, ColumnsPerTable, AvgTBPerDatabase, DocumentMixGBPerDay, S3BigDataGBPerDay); ignores DatabaseCount, StructuredCapacityTB, StructuredTimelineDays, UnstructuredCapacityTB, UnstructuredTimelineDays, DataExcludedPercent, and FullScanPercent.

sourceType.description: Type of source being protected — see the enum values for what each represents.

sourceDataTB.description: Total consumed data (TB) needing protection, before compression. Typical range 0.1–1024 TB; larger values are accepted but uncommon.

itemsCountM.description: Total item (file) count, in millions, across all sources needing protection. Typical range up to 1000 (i.e. up to 1 billion items).

sourcesCount.description: Number of distinct sources (e.g. NAS shares) needing protection. Used, alongside proxy core count, to decide whether the proxy tier should scale out or up.

dailyChangeRatePct.description: Average percentage of the (post-growth) source data set that changes each day, driving the size of daily incremental backups. 0–100; typical values are 0.1–5%.

yoYGrowthPct.description: Annual data growth rate (%), compounded over `ProjectionYears` to project `SourceDataTB`/`ItemsCountM` forward before sizing. 0–100; 0 = no growth.

projectionYears.description: Number of years to project `YoYGrowthPct` growth forward before sizing. 0–100; typical values up to 7.

SourceData.description:
Describes the unstructured data source being protected: its type, volume, item count, and the
compression, change-rate, and growth assumptions used to size it.

SourceType.description:
Type of unstructured data source being protected.

- 0 = Nas: A NAS/file share source, protected file-by-file (Veeam Backup for NAS).
- 1 = Object: An object storage source (e.g. S3-compatible buckets).

An object storage source (e.g. S3-compatible buckets).

StringApiResponse.description:
Documentation-only wrapper describing the shape every controller in this API returns on success:
`Ok(new { success = true, data = result })`. It exists purely so Swashbuckle has a concrete
type to generate a response schema from — anonymous C# types cannot be represented in an OpenAPI
schema, so without this wrapper every 200 response documents as bare "OK" with no body.
            
IMPORTANT: this type is NOT referenced by any controller's actual return statement. Controllers
keep constructing the anonymous `{ success, data }` object at runtime; this class is only
ever used inside `[ProducesResponseType(typeof(ApiResponse<T>), 200)]` attributes to
tell Swagger what that anonymous object looks like. Because the two are not linked by the
compiler, they can silently drift apart: if a controller's anonymous success object ever gains,
loses, or renames a property, this type must be updated by hand to match, or the generated
documentation will describe a response shape the API no longer actually returns.
            
Property names are PascalCase here (standard C# convention) but serialize as camelCase
(`success` / `data`) under this project's default System.Text.Json configuration
(`AddControllers()` with no custom `PropertyNamingPolicy`) — the same casing already
produced by the anonymous object in every controller today, so no `[JsonPropertyName]`
attributes are needed for the documented schema to match the real wire format.

StructuredSizingType.description:
Structured data sizing type

- 0 = Databases: Sizes structured data from DatabaseCount directly.
- 1 = Capacity: Sizes structured data from StructuredCapacityTB, converting it to an equivalent database count via AvgTBPerDatabase (rounded to the nearest whole database) before applying the same per-database column-scan formula as Databases mode.

TapeDrives.description:
Tape drive hardware model used by the Tape and FileToTape calculators to look up native
capacity, native throughput, and available compression ratios for sizing.

- 0 = LTO4: LTO Ultrium generation 4 drive.
- 1 = LTO5: LTO Ultrium generation 5 drive.
- 2 = LTO6: LTO Ultrium generation 6 drive.
- 3 = LTO7: LTO Ultrium generation 7 drive.
- 4 = LTO8_HH_Type_M: LTO Ultrium generation 8 drive, half-height form factor, loaded with Type M8 media (an LTO-7 cartridge reformatted for LTO-8 use). Lower native capacity than a true LTO-8 cartridge.
- 5 = LTO8_FH_Type_M: LTO Ultrium generation 8 drive, full-height form factor, loaded with Type M8 media (an LTO-7 cartridge reformatted for LTO-8 use). Lower native capacity than a true LTO-8 cartridge.
- 6 = LTO8_HH: LTO Ultrium generation 8 drive, half-height form factor, loaded with native LTO-8 media.
- 7 = LTO8_FH: LTO Ultrium generation 8 drive, full-height form factor, loaded with native LTO-8 media — the fastest LTO-8 option modeled here.
- 8 = LTO9: LTO Ultrium generation 9 drive.
- 9 = TS1140: IBM TS1140 enterprise tape drive (3592 media family).
- 10 = TS1150: IBM TS1150 enterprise tape drive (3592 media family) — successor to TS1140 with higher native capacity and speed.
- 11 = TS1155: IBM TS1155 enterprise tape drive (3592 media family) — successor to TS1150.

calculatorMode.description:
Whether the response is floored to `CVariables` minimums (Simple) or returned as raw
calculated values (Advanced). Note: Simple mode's response also drops the `TapesFullOutLib`
and `TapesDailyOutLib` fields present in the Advanced response — see the enum values for
the mode semantics themselves.

siteId.description: Caller-supplied identifier for the site being sized. Not validated; echoed back in the response.

siteName.description: Caller-supplied display name for the site being sized. Echoed back in the response.

id.description: Caller-supplied identifier for this tape job/library configuration. Echoed back in the response as `TapeId`.

tapeName.description: Caller-supplied display name for this tape job/library configuration. Echoed back in the response.

growthRatePercent.description: Annual data growth rate, as a whole-number percentage (e.g. 10 = 10%/year), compounded over `GrowthRateScopeYears`. 0 = no growth.

growthRateScopeYears.description: Number of years over which `GrowthRatePercent` is compounded to project `FullSizeTB`/`DailySizeTB` forward. 0 = no projection.

tapeDriveType.description: Tape drive hardware model used to look up native capacity, speed, and available compression ratios — see the enum values.

compressability.description: Assumed compression ratio applied to the drive's native capacity — see the enum values. Ignored when `CustomCompression` is set above 1.

customCompression.description: Custom compression multiplier applied to the drive's native capacity, overriding `Compressability` when greater than 1 (e.g. 1.8 = 1.8x native capacity). 1 = no override, use `Compressability` instead.

effectiveTapeSpeedPct.description: Real-world tape drive throughput as a percentage of the drive's rated native speed, accounting for overhead. Typically 80-90; default 85.

fullSizeTB.description: Size, in TB, of one full backup before growth is applied.

fullDaysPerWeek.description: How many days per week a full backup runs (e.g. 1 = one full per week).

fullRetentionInLibWeeks.description: Number of weeks a full backup's tape(s) must remain in the library before being shipped out or recycled.

fullBackupWindowHours.description: Hours available in the backup window to complete a full backup to tape. Drives the number of tape drives required.

fullRetentionOutLibWeeks.description: Number of additional weeks a full backup's tape(s) are retained after being shipped out of the library (offsite retention). Only reflected in the Advanced response.

dailySizeTB.description: Size, in TB, of one daily (incremental) backup before growth is applied.

dailyDaysPerWeek.description: How many days per week a daily (incremental) backup runs (e.g. 7 = every day).

dailyRetentionInLibWeeks.description: Number of weeks a daily backup's tape(s) must remain in the library before being shipped out or recycled.

dailyBackupWindowHours.description: Hours available in the backup window to complete a daily backup to tape. Drives the number of tape drives required.

dailyRetentionOutLibWeeks.description: Number of additional weeks a daily backup's tape(s) are retained after being shipped out of the library (offsite retention). Only reflected in the Advanced response.

spareWeeks.description: Extra weeks of spare tapes to hold in the library on top of the calculated in-library requirement, as a buffer.

exportSlots.description: Number of library slots reserved for tapes that are in transit (being exported to or imported from offsite storage), added on top of the in-library tape count.

TapeInputObject.description:
Request body for `/Tape` — sizes image-based (VM/agent) backup-to-tape infrastructure:
tape drives, tapes in-library and shipped out, and the tape server itself.

siteId.description: Echo of the request's `SiteId`, or a freshly generated GUID if none was supplied.

siteName.description: Echo of the request's `SiteName`.

tapeId.description: Echo of the request's `Id`, or a freshly generated GUID if none was supplied.

fullBackupSizeTB.description: Echo of the request's `FullSizeTB` — the un-projected full-backup size, in TB.

dailyBackupSizeTB.description: Echo of the request's `DailySizeTB` — the un-projected daily-backup size, in TB.

projectedFullSizeTB.description: Full-backup size in TB after applying `GrowthRatePercent` compounded over `GrowthRateScopeYears`.

projectedDailySizeTB.description: Daily-backup size in TB after applying `GrowthRatePercent` compounded over `GrowthRateScopeYears`.

inLibBackupSizeTB.description: Total backup data, in TB, that must be kept on tapes held in the library (not yet shipped out).

outLibBackupSizeTB.description: Total backup data, in TB, held on tapes shipped out of the library for the configured out-of-library retention window.

tapeDrivesNeeded.description: Number of physical tape drives needed to complete both the full and daily backups within their configured backup windows.

tapesInLib.description: Number of tapes that must remain in the library to hold the in-library full and daily retention, plus spare tapes for `SpareWeeks`.

tapesTotal.description: Total tapes required — in-library plus shipped-out-of-library — across the full retention scheme.

slotsTotal.description: Total library slots required: tapes held in-library plus the configured `ExportSlots` reserved for tapes in transit.

tapesFullperWeek.description: Tapes consumed per week to hold one week's worth of full backups.

tapesDailyperWeek.description: Tapes consumed per week to hold one week's worth of daily (incremental) backups.

tapesperWeek.description: Total tapes consumed per week (fulls plus dailies).

tapeServerCores.description: CPU cores recommended for the tape server component of this deployment.

tapeServerRam.description: RAM, in GB, recommended for the tape server component of this deployment.

tapesFullOutLib.description: Tapes shipped out of the library to satisfy the full-backup out-of-library retention window (`FullRetentionOutLibWeeks`).

tapesDailyOutLib.description: Tapes shipped out of the library to satisfy the daily-backup out-of-library retention window (`DailyRetentionOutLibWeeks`).

TapeReturnObject.description:
`/Tape` response shape returned when the request's `CalculatorMode` is Advanced.
            Adds `TapesFullOutLib` and `TapesDailyOutLib` to the fields in `TapeReturnBaseObject`.
