Throughput.description: Inbound/outbound network throughput required for a compute resource or disk volume.

performanceTierTransactions.description: API transaction volume for the performance tier repository.

capacityTierTransactions.description: API transaction volume for the capacity tier repository.

archiveTierTransactions.description: API transaction volume for the archive tier repository.

TotalObjectTransactions.description: Object storage API transaction volume, broken out by tier.

offloadMonth.description:
Number of months of retention hold that elapse before the first batch of aged-out restore
points offloads into this tier and starts generating transactions. 0 when data is copied
(or moved) into the tier immediately rather than after a delay — see `OffloadType`.

offloadType.description:
Which restore-point cadence drives data into this tier: `"I"` immediate/incremental
copy, `"W"` weekly GFS points, `"M"` monthly GFS points, or `"Y"` yearly GFS
points (archive-tier results can combine monthly and yearly as `"MY"`). `"-"`
means this tier isn't in use, and all transaction figures on this result are 0.

firstTransactions.description:
Estimated transactions for the tier's first year: the initial full data transfer plus that
year's incremental changes.

secondTransactions.description:
Estimated transactions for the second year, with one year of growth compounded in; equal to
`FirstTransactions` when the configured growth period is shorter than 2 years.

lastTransactions.description:
Estimated transactions for the final year of the configured growth period, with the full
growth rate compounded across the whole period.

firstImmutTrans.description:
Additional first-year transactions from periodic immutability re-lock operations, added on
top of `FirstTransactions` when immutability is enabled for this tier. 0 when
immutability isn't enabled.

secondImmutTrans.description:
Additional second-year transactions from periodic immutability re-lock operations, added on
top of `SecondTransactions` when immutability is enabled for this tier. 0 when
immutability isn't enabled.

lastImmutTrans.description:
Additional final-year transactions from periodic immutability re-lock operations, added on
top of `LastTransactions` when immutability is enabled for this tier. 0 when
immutability isn't enabled.

TransactionResult.description:
Estimated object-storage API transaction volume (in thousands) for one backup-storage tier
(performance, capacity, or archive) over the sizing scenario's projected lifetime: the first
year, a second year, and the final year of the configured growth period. Each period also has
an immutability-adjusted counterpart (`FirstImmutTrans`/`SecondImmutTrans`/
`LastImmutTrans`) covering the extra transactions periodic immutability re-lock
operations add on top of the plain figure for that same period when immutability is enabled
for the tier. A tier with no offload configured reports all-zero figures with
`OffloadType` of `"-"`.

enabled.description: Whether this workload is included when sizing is run across a batch of workloads.

siteID.description: Identifier of the site this workload belongs to.

sourceTB.description: Source data volume (TB) for this workload.

dataPropertyID.description: Identifier of the data property/profile definition associated with this workload.

backup.description: Reference to this workload's backup job (retention, repository, and backup window).

copiesEnabled.description: Whether backup copy jobs are enabled for this workload.

copies.description: Reference to this workload's backup copy job (retention, repository, and backup window).

ioControl.description: Identifier of the I/O control/throttling profile applied to this workload.

calculatorMode.description:
Controls how much of the sizing result is returned: Advanced returns the full breakdown
(proxy, cache, gateway, secondary, and archive sizing in addition to the primary repository),
while Simple returns only `SourceData`, `Helper`, and `PrimaryRepo` — the
underlying calculation and its values are identical in both modes.

sourceData.description: Source data volume, item count, compression, change rate, and growth assumptions used to size this workload.

primaryCopy.description: Primary backup repository, retention, and backup window settings.

secondaryCopies.description: Secondary (backup copy) repositories, one entry per additional copy. Omit or leave null for no secondary copies.

archiveCopy.description: Archive repository, retention, and copy/move mode settings.

blockGenerationDays.description:
Number of days of immutable data blocks generated before old blocks can be reused on an
immutable object storage repository. Added to the effective retention window when
immutability is enabled on the primary, secondary, or archive repository.

useNativeCft.description: Whether to size the cache repository using Veeam's native CFT (Changed File Tracking) sizing, which requires substantially less cache compute/RAM than the legacy sizing formula.

UnstructuredInputObject.description:
Request payload for NAS/file share (unstructured data) backup sizing. Carries both the
VSE-compatible workload identity/envelope fields (`VSE_Compat` region below — used to
identify and correlate this workload when it's part of a larger multi-workload request) and
the actual sizing inputs (`SourceData`, `PrimaryCopy`, `SecondaryCopies`,
`ArchiveCopy`) that drive the calculation.

sourceData.description: Source data settings used for this calculation (volume, item count, compression, change rate, growth).

helper.description: Intermediate sizing figures — provided for visibility into how the results below were derived, not required for sizing infrastructure.

primaryRepo.description: Capacity, API call volume, and network requirements for the primary backup repository.

proxy.description: Compute (cores/RAM) required for the backup proxy that reads source data and writes it to the primary repository.

proxyLayout.description: Recommendation on whether to scale the proxy tier out (add proxies) or up (larger proxies) first, based on proxy core count versus source count.

cacheRepo.description: Compute (cores/RAM) and cache storage required for the cache repository (metadata/CFT cache) that sits alongside the primary backup repository.

repoGtw.description: Compute (cores/RAM) and staging storage required for the repository gateway server that fronts an object storage primary repository.

secondaryRepos.description: Capacity, API call volume, and network requirements for each configured secondary (backup copy) repository, one entry per `SecondaryCopies` in the request.

archiveRepo.description: Capacity, API call volume, and network requirements for the archive repository, covering both copy mode (a separate archive copy) and move mode (data moved after primary retention expires).

UnstructuredReturnObject.description:
Full sizing result for the Unstructured (NAS/file share) calculator, returned when
`CalculatorMode` is Advanced. In Simple mode only `SourceData`, `Helper`, and
`PrimaryRepo` are populated — see `UnstructuredReturnSimpleObject`.

returned as HTTP 400 with a `ReturnMessage` body instead.

data.description: The calculated result payload for this endpoint.

vb365GroupCount.description: Number of Microsoft 365 groups being monitored.

vb365Sites.description: Number of SharePoint sites being monitored.

vb365Teams.description: Number of Microsoft Teams being monitored.

compute sizing on every request, regardless of `Mode`.

vb365ReposPerServer.description: Average number of backup repositories per VB365 server.

vb365ProxyPerServer.description: Average number of backup proxies per VB365 server.

vb365BackupJobTotal.description: Total number of VB365 backup jobs being monitored.

vb365TotalJobsPerDay.description: Average number of VB365 backup jobs that run per day.

- 0 = Mailbox: Exchange Online mailbox.
- 1 = Archive: Exchange Online in-place/personal archive mailbox. Sized the same way as a mailbox but tracked as a separate object group.

hvInputs.description: Hyper-V infrastructure counts to monitor — see `HvInputs`. Optional; only relevant when the environment includes Hyper-V hosts.

vbrInputs.description: Veeam Backup & Replication infrastructure counts to monitor — see `VbrInputs`. Required (non-null) on every request regardless of `Mode`.

historicalDataPeriodMonthsB53.description:
Number of months of historical performance data Veeam ONE retains for the monitored
infrastructure. Directly drives the configuration database size — more months retained
means a larger database.

eventsDataPeriodMonthsB54.description:
Number of months of historical events/alarms data Veeam ONE retains for the monitored
infrastructure. Contributes to the configuration database size alongside
`HistoricalDataPeriodMonthsB53`.

vB365Input.description: Veeam Backup for Microsoft 365 infrastructure counts to monitor — see `VB365Inputs`. Required (non-null) on every request regardless of `Mode`.

VeeamOneInput.description:
Input for sizing a Veeam ONE monitoring deployment's configuration database and server
compute requirements. `Mode` selects which infrastructure category (VMware, vCloud
Director, Hyper-V, VBR, or VB365) drives the database-size calculation — populate the
matching nested input(s) for the selected mode. `VmWareInputs`, `VbrInputs`, and
`VB365Input` must all be supplied (non-null, though their fields may be zero) on every
request regardless of `Mode`, because the Veeam ONE server's own compute sizing always
factors VM/host and backup/VB365-server counts.

siteId.description: Identifier for the site this result belongs to. Not populated from the request — always a freshly generated value.

siteName.description: Display name for the site this result belongs to. Not populated from the request — always empty.

id.description: Identifier for this sizing entry. Not populated from the request — always a freshly generated value.

name.description: Display name for this sizing entry. Not populated from the request — always empty.

totalDbSizeGb.description: Configuration database storage required by the Veeam ONE server, in GB.

oneServerCompute.description:
Compute (CPU cores, RAM) and disk volumes required to run the Veeam ONE server, sized
from the monitored infrastructure counts. Always includes a 50 GB cache volume plus a
configuration-database volume matching `TotalDbSizeGb`.

VeeamOneReturnObject.description:
Result of sizing a Veeam ONE monitoring deployment — the configuration database storage
requirement and the compute (cores, RAM, disk) needed for the Veeam ONE server itself.

success.description:
Whether the calculation succeeded. Always `true` for a 200 response — failures are
returned as HTTP 400 with a `ReturnMessage` body instead.

data.description: The calculated result payload for this endpoint.

VeeamOneReturnObjectApiResponse.description:
Documentation-only wrapper describing the shape every controller in this API returns on success:
`Ok(new { success = true, data = result })`. It exists purely so Swashbuckle has a concrete
type to generate a response schema from — anonymous C# types cannot be represented in an OpenAPI
schema, so without this wrapper every 200 response documents as bare "OK" with no body.
            
IMPORTANT: this type is NOT referenced by any controller's actual return statement. Controllers
keep constructing the anonymous `{ success, data }` object at runtime; this class is only
ever used inside `[ProducesResponseType(typeof(ApiResponse<T>), 200)]` attributes to
tell Swagger what that anonymous object looks like. Because the two are not linked by the
compiler, they can silently drift apart: if a controller's anonymous success object ever gains,
loses, or renames a property, this type must be updated by hand to match, or the generated
documentation will describe a response shape the API no longer actually returns.
            
Property names are PascalCase here (standard C# convention) but serialize as camelCase
(`success` / `data`) under this project's default System.Text.Json configuration
(`AddControllers()` with no custom `PropertyNamingPolicy`) — the same casing already
produced by the anonymous object in every controller today, so no `[JsonPropertyName]`
attributes are needed for the documented schema to match the real wire format.

productVersion.description:
Which VBR product version to size for — see the enum values for what each version changes.
v12 sizing rules remain available for existing installations; new installations use v13.

calculatorMode.description:
Whether calculated sizing is floored to `CVariables`' documented minimums (Simple) or
returned as raw, unfloored values (Advanced) — see the enum values for details.

siteId.description: Caller-supplied identifier for the site this workload belongs to. Not echoed into the response.

siteName.description: Caller-supplied display name for the site this workload belongs to. Not echoed into the response.

id.description: Caller-supplied identifier for this workload entry. Not echoed into the response.

name.description: Caller-supplied display name for this workload entry. Not echoed into the response.

growthRatePercent.description:
Annual growth rate, as a whole-number percentage (e.g. 10 = 10%/year). Used directly to
project source size for proxy compute sizing, and — when `growthFactor` is left at 0 —
also supplies the growth rate used for performance/capacity/archive tier storage sizing.
0 = no growth.

growthRateScopeYears.description:
Number of years `GrowthRatePercent` is compounded over for proxy compute sizing. When
`projectLength` is left at 0, this also supplies the forecast horizon used for storage
sizing. 0 disables growth projection.

hyperVisor.description: Hypervisor platform hosting the protected workload(s). Affects proxy compute sizing — see the enum values for specifics.

sourceTB.description: Source data volume, in TB, to be backed up. The primary driver of proxy, repository, and storage sizing.

changeRate.description: Daily change rate, as a percentage of source data, used to size incremental restore points and proxy/repository ingest throughput.

reduction.description: Compression/deduplication reduction, as a "compress BY" percentage (not "compress TO"), applied to the full backup size before sizing storage and bandwidth.

days.description:
Number of daily (short-term, non-GFS) restore points to retain on the performance tier. An
extra 7 days is added internally before sizing, except when `objectStorage` is enabled
or when this input is used for a VM replication calculation.

blockCloning.description: Whether to size storage assuming VBR's block cloning (fast clone) feature is used for GFS full backups, reducing the incremental storage needed for full restore points.

backupWindowHours.description: Backup window, in hours, available to complete the incremental backup. Used with `sourceTB`/`changeRate`/`reduction` to size the proxy and repository throughput (Mbps) needed to finish within the window.

showPoints.description: Whether to include the intermediate per-restore-point breakdown in the response.

growthFactor.description:
Annual growth rate, as a percentage, applied when sizing performance/capacity/archive tier
storage and GFS restore points. Defaults to `GrowthRatePercent` when left at 0.

largeBlock.description: Whether to size backups assuming VBR's large block format rather than the standard block size — increases both storage and object-storage transaction sizing.

projectLength.description:
Forecast horizon in years over which `growthFactor` is compounded for storage sizing.
Defaults to `GrowthRateScopeYears` when left at 0.

weeklies.description: Number of weekly (Grandfather-Father-Son) restore points to retain, in addition to the daily points in `days`.

monthlies.description: Number of monthly GFS restore points to retain, in addition to the daily and weekly points.

yearlies.description: Number of yearly GFS restore points to retain, in addition to the daily, weekly, and monthly points.

capacityTierDays.description:
Number of days a restore point remains on the performance tier before it's eligible to move
to the capacity tier. Enforced to a minimum of 7 days, and rounded up internally to align
with the weekly GFS point schedule.

archiveTierDays.description: Number of days a GFS restore point remains on the capacity tier (or, when there's no capacity tier, on the performance tier) before it's eligible to move to the archive tier.

archiveTierEnabled.description:
Whether an archive tier is configured for this workload. When enabled with no capacity tier
(`moveCapacityTierEnabled`/`copyCapacityTierEnabled` both false) and `objectStorage`
is true, GFS points move directly from the performance tier to the archive tier once they age
past `archiveTierDays`.

archiveTierStandalone.description:
Whether the archive tier is sized as an independent copy job rather than continuing the
performance/capacity tier's incremental chain. When true, each archived restore point is
sized as a full backup rather than using `changeRate`.

moveCapacityTierEnabled.description:
Whether aged restore points are moved (not just copied) from the performance tier to the
capacity tier — once a point ages past `capacityTierDays`/the GFS hold window it's
retained only on the capacity tier and no longer counted on the performance tier.

copyCapacityTierEnabled.description: Whether restore points are copied to a capacity tier repository in addition to being retained on the performance tier.

backupType.description: Whether this workload's job is a standalone backup job or a backup copy job — see the enum values.

copiesEnabled.description: Whether backup copy jobs are enabled for this workload.

immutablePerf.description:
Whether immutability is enabled on the performance tier repository. When true (and the
performance tier is object storage), adds `PerformanceTierImmutabilityTaxGB` of extra
retained storage, sized from `immutablePerfDays` plus `BlockGenerationDays`.

immutablePerfDays.description: Number of days performance-tier restore points are locked immutable. Drives `PerformanceTierImmutabilityTaxGB` together with `BlockGenerationDays` when `immutablePerf` is enabled.

immutableCap.description:
Whether immutability is enabled on the capacity tier repository. When true (with a capacity
tier configured), adds `CapacityTierImmutabilityTaxGB` of extra retained storage, sized
from `immutableCapDays` plus `BlockGenerationDays`, and locks capacity/archive tier
GFS points as immutable.

immutableCapDays.description: Number of days capacity-tier restore points are locked immutable. Drives `CapacityTierImmutabilityTaxGB` together with `BlockGenerationDays` when `immutableCap` is enabled.

blockGenerationDays.description:
Number of days of immutable data blocks generated before old blocks can be reused on an
immutable object storage repository. Added to the applicable immutability period when
calculating `PerformanceTierImmutabilityTaxGB`/`CapacityTierImmutabilityTaxGB`.

storageType.description: Repository storage type for the performance tier. Setting this to the literal string "object" enables the same object-storage immutability-tax sizing as `objectStorage`, independently of that flag.

repoId.description: Identifier of the repository this workload's performance-tier backups are stored in.

repoName.description: Display name of the repository this workload's performance-tier backups are stored in.

capTierRepoId.description: Identifier of the repository this workload's capacity-tier backups are stored in.

archiveTierRepoId.description: Identifier of the repository this workload's archive-tier backups are stored in.

objectStorage.description:
Whether the performance tier repository is object storage rather than block storage. Affects
proxy ingest throughput assumptions and GFS point sizing, and disables the workspace/merge-area
calculation (`WorkspaceGB` is always 0 when true).

enableEntropyScanning.description: Whether data entropy scanning is factored into proxy sizing. Only takes effect when `preferPhysicalProxySizing` is true; increases the cores/RAM the proxy needs per unit of incremental throughput.

preferPhysicalProxySizing.description: Whether to size the backup proxy assuming physical proxy hardware — with throughput assumptions that vary by target storage type and `enableEntropyScanning` — instead of the flat virtual-proxy throughput assumption.

indexOptions.description: Guest file-system indexing sizing inputs — see `IndexingInputObject`.

VmAgentInputs.description:
Request body for `/VmAgent` and `/Mongo` — sizes physical/agent-based backup proxy
compute and performance/capacity/archive tier storage, including GFS restore points and
estimated object-storage API transaction volume, for a single workload.

vmCountVmwareB5.description: Number of VMware virtual machines being monitored.

hostCountVmwareB6.description: Number of VMware ESXi hosts being monitored.

dataStoresB7.description: Number of VMware datastores being monitored.

resourcePoolsB8.description: Number of VMware resource pools being monitored.

clustersB9.description: Number of VMware clusters being monitored.

vAppsB10.description: Number of VMware vApps being monitored.

vCenterCount.description: Number of vCenter Server instances being monitored.

avgStorageAdapterPerHostB12.description: Average number of storage adapters per ESXi host.

avgStoragePathPerHostB13.description: Average number of storage paths per ESXi host.

avgNicsPerHostB14.description: Average number of network interface cards (NICs) per ESXi host.

avgDatastoresPerOneVmB17.description: Average number of datastores used per virtual machine.

avgVirtualDisksPerVmB18.description: Average number of virtual disks per virtual machine.

avgGuestDiskPerVmB19.description: Average number of guest OS disks/partitions per virtual machine.

avgNicsPerVmB20.description: Average number of network interface cards (NICs) per virtual machine.

VmWareInputs.description:
VMware vSphere infrastructure inventory that Veeam ONE will monitor. Used when `Mode`
is `Typical` or `Advanced` (full detail) or `BackupOnly` (a reduced subset of
these counts).

throughputMbps.description: Inbound/outbound network throughput required for this disk.

diskPurpose.description: What this disk volume is used for — see the enum values.

Volume.description: A single disk volume required for a compute resource, sized for a specific storage purpose.

tags.name: BackupServer
tags.description: VBR server, proxies, repos, and storage tier sizing (performance/capacity/archive).

tags.name: Mongo
tags.description: MongoDB-related sizing (uses the same input shape as VmAgent).
